modules.notes: Human-consent authorization gateway for AI agents. Pnpm + Turborepo monorepo. Next.js 16 App Router + TypeScript + Prisma SQLite. 9 packages. Key concept: AI agents cannot execute privileged actions without a step-up human consent challenge, every action logged to signed ledger. TypeScript compiles clean. No test suite. Auth0 not configured. vault-client and google-executor not production-ready. dev.db on disk confirms Prisma migrations ran locally.

modules.notes: Auth0 AI Agent Hackathon submission. Standalone Next.js 14 + Auth0 SDK v4. 8-stage gateway: intercept → policy → risk → step-up → consent → RFC 8693 token exchange → execute → audit. Distinct from consentchain monorepo (which has Auth0 as placeholder only). Real implementation: policy engine, audit store, rate limiter, component inventory manifest. TypeScript compiles clean. Tests blocked by ERR_REQUIRE_ESM vitest config gap (same class as agent-sentinel). HANDOFF (2026-04-06) confirms tests passed at last checkpoint.

modules.label: HIDRS — Instructional Dependency Study
modules.short_description: Local-first N-of-1 research system for measuring instructional dependency opacity, prioritization load, cognitive switching cost, and assessment-alignment risk in online courses.

modules.next_required_action: Copy workbook from Google Drive to workbook/ directory. Run validate_workbook.py. Enter retrospective data for Course 1 Lessons 1–3. Begin Course 1 Lesson 4 prospective logging.
modules.notes: N-of-1 observational study. Researcher is sole participant. Mixed design: retrospective capture for Course 1 Lessons 1–3 (completed before study began), prospective logging from Course 1 Lesson 4 forward. 6 courses, 38 total lessons. validate_repo.py PASS at initial commit. Workbook exists in Google Drive but not yet placed in workbook/ directory — validate_workbook.py returns PARTIAL (expected). No prospective data collected yet. Local-first. No deployment. No generalizability claimed. Governed as TLC 2.0 module_library + documentation + evidence-producing study system.

modules.notes: First version of the TLC reusable research repo template. Derived from HIDRS instance pattern. Includes the I8 Visual Understanding Layer invariant as a hard nonnegotiable enforced by validate_repo.py. Six Python scripts, five JSON schemas, six markdown templates, nine docs, and six Mermaid/pictograph visual files. Creation script copies and substitutes placeholders, inits git, runs validator. HIDRS at hidrs-instructional-dependency-study is the reference instance.
